frameworks.name: sampling-based probabilistic representation
frameworks.one_line: cortical population activity patterns are samples from an inferred posterior distribution

frameworks.name: probabilistic inference in cortex
frameworks.one_line: visual cortex performs Bayesian inference over latent causes of stimuli

frameworks.name: Poisson-like / unstructured variability models
frameworks.one_line: prior accounts ignore variability or treat it as unstructured noise

core_question: Can the magnitude and structure of cortical response variability be explained as samples from a probabilistic inference performed by the visual cortex?
key_assumptions: population activity patterns correspond to statistical samples from a posterior
stimulus uncertainty has a representational signature in neural responses
manipulations: stimulus presence vs. absence (driven vs. spontaneous activity)
stimulus properties that modulate perceptual uncertainty
comparison across noise, signal, and spontaneous variability regimes
key_findings: structured stimulus-dependent variability in V1 matches sampling predictions
noise, signal, and spontaneous variability and correlations are jointly accounted for
perceptual uncertainty is encoded by response variability, not the mean
interpretations: cortical variability is a computational signature of probabilistic inference rather than nuisance noise
a sampling-based code unifies multiple variability phenomena in primary visual cortex
